edges.description: Unresolved meiotic recombination intermediates block progression through prophase I, leading to germ-cell arrest and depletion.
edges.target: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: In 46,XX individuals, germ-cell depletion exhausts the ovarian follicle pool and produces primary ovarian insufficiency / ovarian dysgenesis.
edges.source: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion
edges.target: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In male mice the same germ-cell depletion produces spermatogenic failure; an analogous male branch is predicted in humans but not yet reported.
edges.source: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: The female branch presents clinically as primary / premature ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Ovarian failure produces hypergonadotropic hypogonadism with elevated gonadotropins and low anti-Mullerian hormone.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Severe ovarian dysgenesis in reported individuals manifests on imaging as absent bilateral ovaries.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Hypoestrogenism from ovarian failure delays development of secondary sexual characteristics.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Ovarian failure delays menarche in the reported affected girls.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Loss of ovarian feedback leads to elevated circulating follicle-stimulating hormone.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Loss of ovarian feedback leads to elevated circulating luteinizing hormone.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: Ovarian follicle depletion is reflected by decreased circulating anti-Mullerian hormone.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

nodes.description: Pelvic imaging in reported individuals showed absent bilateral ovaries with a cord-like uterus, consistent with ovarian dysgenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: pelvic ultrasound showed a cord-like uterus and absence of bilateral ovaries.

nodes.description: Circulating anti-Mullerian hormone was decreased, consistent with depleted ovarian reserve in primary ovarian insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: The laboratory test results indicated increased follicle-stimulating hormone and luteinizing hormone, decreased anti-Mullerian hormone, and pelvic ultrasound showed a cord-like uterus and absence of bilateral ovaries.

nodes.description: Delayed menarche was the presenting complaint in the reported HROB family.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: A 13-year and 6-month-old girl attended the Hunan Children's Hospital due to delayed menarche.

nodes.description: Affected girls presented with delayed menarche and absent secondary sexual characteristics until hormone replacement was started.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Oral estradiol valerate at an initial dose of 0.125 mg/d was given to the proband, and the secondary sexual characteristics began to develop after 6 months.

nodes.description: Affected girls had increased follicle-stimulating hormone, reflecting loss of ovarian feedback in primary ovarian insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: The laboratory test results indicated increased follicle-stimulating hormone and luteinizing hormone, decreased anti-Mullerian hormone, and pelvic ultrasound showed a cord-like uterus and absence of bilateral ovaries.

nodes.description: Affected girls had increased luteinizing hormone, reflecting hypergonadotropic ovarian failure.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: The laboratory test results indicated increased follicle-stimulating hormone and luteinizing hormone, decreased anti-Mullerian hormone, and pelvic ultrasound showed a cord-like uterus and absence of bilateral ovaries.

evidence.reference_title: Meiotic genes in premature ovarian insufficiency: variants in HROB and REC8 as likely genetic causes.
evidence.snippet: HROB encodes a factor that recruits MCM8/9 for DNA damage repair.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: carried heterozygous variants of HROB gene c.718C>T (p.Arg240*) and c.1351C>T (p.Arg451*), which were inherited from their parents respectively and consistent with autosomal recessive inheritance.

nodes.description: Ovarian failure is accompanied by hypergonadotropic hypogonadism with elevated follicle-stimulating and luteinizing hormone and low anti-Mullerian hormone.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: The laboratory test results indicated increased follicle-stimulating hormone and luteinizing hormone, decreased anti-Mullerian hormone, and pelvic ultrasound showed a cord-like uterus and absence of bilateral ovaries.

nodes.description: HROB is the OB-fold factor that recruits and activates the MCM8-MCM9 helicase at sites of DNA damage to drive recombination-associated DNA synthesis. Biallelic HROB loss therefore disables MCM8-MCM9-dependent homologous recombination, leaving meiotic and repair-associated DNA breaks unresolved.

biological_processes: double-strand break repair via homologous recombination

evidence.reference_title: Control of homologous recombination by the HROB-MCM8-MCM9 pathway.
evidence.snippet: C17orf53/HROB is an OB-fold-containing factor involved in HR that acts by recruiting the MCM8-MCM9 helicase to sites of DNA damage to promote DNA synthesis.

evidence.reference_title: MCM8IP activates the MCM8-9 helicase to promote DNA synthesis and homologous recombination upon DNA damage.
evidence.snippet: MCM8IP directly associates with MCM8-9, a helicase complex mutated in primary ovarian insufficiency, and RPA1.

evidence.reference_title: MCM8IP activates the MCM8-9 helicase to promote DNA synthesis and homologous recombination upon DNA damage.
evidence.snippet: MCM8IP stimulates the helicase activity of MCM8-9.

nodes.description: Loss of oocytes during meiotic progression depletes the ovarian reserve, presenting clinically as primary ovarian insufficiency, ovarian dysgenesis, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism in 46,XX individuals.
nodes.id: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Meiotic genes in premature ovarian insufficiency: variants in HROB and REC8 as likely genetic causes.
evidence.snippet: In silico analyses, combined with concordant mouse model phenotypes support these as new genetic causes of POI.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of novel pathogenic gene HROB in a family with primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: both girls were diagnosed with primary ovarian insufficiency.

nodes.description: Affected 46,XX individuals present with primary / premature ovarian insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Meiotic genes in premature ovarian insufficiency: variants in HROB and REC8 as likely genetic causes.
evidence.snippet: propose HROB and REC8 variants as new genetic causes while exploring their link to pathogenesis.

nodes.description: Germ cells that cannot complete homologous recombination arrest during meiotic prophase I and are eliminated rather than maturing into functional gametes, depleting the germ-cell pool.
nodes.id: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

evidence.reference_title: Control of homologous recombination by the HROB-MCM8-MCM9 pathway.
evidence.snippet: Mice with targeted mutations in Hrob are infertile due to depletion of germ cells and display phenotypes consistent with a prophase I meiotic arrest.

nodes.description: In male mice, HROB loss depletes germ cells with seminiferous tubules largely devoid of germ cells, predicting a spermatogenic-failure branch in 46,XY humans. No human male case has yet been reported, so this node rests on model-organism evidence only.

evidence.reference_title: Control of homologous recombination by the HROB-MCM8-MCM9 pathway.
evidence.snippet: Mice with targeted mutations in Hrob are infertile due to depletion of germ cells and display phenotypes consistent with a prophase I meiotic arrest.
